subject: math
title: Grade 3 Math — EOG Practice Test 3
instructions: Read each question carefully. Choose the best answer, type your answer in the box, or fill in the blank to make the equation true. You may not use a calculator.

questions.questionText: The array shows seashells arranged in equal rows. How many seashells are there in all?
questions.explanation: 4 rows of 6 seashells is 4 × 6 = 24.

questions.questionText: What is 7 × 9?
Mr. Lee shares 48 crayons equally among 6 students. How many crayons does each student get?
questions.explanation: 48 ÷ 6 = 8 crayons for each student.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
Nico knows that 4 × 7 = 28. Which fact can help him find 5 × 7?
questions.explanation: 5 × 7 is one more group of 7 than 4 × 7, so 28 + 7 = 35. This uses the distributive property: 5 × 7 = (4 × 7) + (1 × 7).

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
Sofia buys 7 boxes of pencils. Each box has 8 pencils. She gives 12 pencils to her teacher. How many pencils does Sofia have left?
questions.explanation: First 7 × 8 = 56 pencils. Then 56 − 12 = 44 pencils left. The distractor 56 is the total before giving any away.

questions.questionText: Devon has 5 bags with 7 oranges in each bag. He buys 9 more oranges. How many oranges does he have now? Write only the number.
questions.explanation: 5 × 7 = 35 oranges, then 35 + 9 = 44 oranges.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
questions.explanation: 6 × 4 = 24, and 48 ÷ 2 = 24, so the blank is 48.

questions.questionText: Select ALL numbers that are EVEN.
questions.explanation: A number is even if its ones digit is 0, 2, 4, 6, or 8. 36 and 48 are even. 51 and 27 are odd.

questions.questionText: Select ALL expressions that are equal to 24.
A coach divides 72 players into teams of 9 players each. How many teams are there? Write only the number.
questions.explanation: 72 ÷ 9 = 8 teams.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
questions.explanation: 7 × 5 = 35, and 35 = 20 + 15, so the blank is 20.

questions.questionText: What is 374 + 248?
A library had 612 books. Students borrowed 287 books. How many books are left?
What number do these base-ten blocks show?
questions.explanation: 4 hundreds, 3 tens, and 0 ones make 430.

questions.questionText: What is 748 rounded to the nearest hundred?
questions.explanation: To round to the nearest hundred, look at the tens digit. It is 4, so round DOWN: 748 rounds to 700. Do not be tricked by the 8 in the ones place.

questions.questionText: The table shows tickets sold each day. How many tickets were sold in all three days? Write only the number.
questions.explanation: 128 + 215 + 134 = 477 tickets in all.

questions.questionText: What fraction of the bar is shaded?
questions.explanation: 5 of the 8 equal parts are shaded, so 5/8.

questions.questionText: Which fraction does the point on the number line show?
questions.explanation: The line from 0 to 1 is split into 5 equal parts. The point is at the second mark, which is 2/5.

questions.questionText: On a number line from 0 to 1, Point R is at 1/2 and Point S is at 1/3. Which statement is true?
questions.explanation: With a numerator of 1, the fraction with the SMALLER denominator is larger. Halves are bigger than thirds, so 1/2 > 1/3, and R is to the right of S.

questions.questionText: Which fraction is equivalent to 1/2?
questions.explanation: Bar B shows 4 of 8 parts shaded, the same amount as 1 of 2. So 4/8 = 1/2.

questions.questionText: Select ALL comparisons that are TRUE.
questions.explanation: 5/6 > 2/6 (same denominator, bigger numerator). 4/4 = 1 (all parts shaded). 2/8 < 2/6 (eighths are smaller than sixths). But 1/3 < 1/2, so that one is false.

questions.questionText: What fraction of the circle is shaded?
questions.explanation: 1 of the 6 equal parts is shaded, so 1/6.

questions.questionText: Which fraction is equal to the whole number 1?
questions.explanation: When all 4 of 4 equal parts are shaded, the whole circle is filled. 4/4 = 1.

questions.questionText: There are 18 grapes on a plate. Maya eats 1/3 of them. How many grapes does Maya eat? Write only the number.
questions.explanation: To find 1/3 of 18, split into 3 equal groups: 18 ÷ 3 = 6 grapes.

questions.questionText: Select ALL fractions that are equal to 1/3.
questions.explanation: 2/6 and 3/9 both equal 1/3. But 2/3 is more than 1/3, and 1/6 is less than 1/3.

questions.questionText: Leo ate 3/4 of a small sandwich. Priya ate 3/8 of a same-size sandwich. Who ate more?
questions.explanation: Both ate 3 pieces, but fourths are bigger than eighths, so 3/4 > 3/8. Leo ate more.

questions.questionText: Which fraction does the point on the number line show?
questions.explanation: The line from 0 to 1 is split into 3 equal parts. The point is at the first mark, which is 1/3.

questions.questionText: What is the area of this rectangle?
questions.explanation: Area = length × width = 7 × 3 = 21 square cm.

questions.questionText: A rectangle is 8 feet long and 3 feet wide. What is its PERIMETER?
questions.explanation: Perimeter = 8 + 3 + 8 + 3 = 22 ft. The distractor 24 is the AREA (8 × 3), a common area-vs-perimeter mix-up.

questions.questionText: A square rug has sides that are each 7 feet long. What is the perimeter of the rug, in feet? Write only the number.
questions.explanation: A square has 4 equal sides. Perimeter = 7 + 7 + 7 + 7 = 28 feet.

questions.questionText: Music class starts at 9:50. It lasts 25 minutes. What time does music class end?
questions.explanation: 9:50 + 25 minutes crosses the hour. 9:50 + 10 = 10:00, then 15 more minutes makes 10:15.

questions.questionText: What time does the clock show?
questions.explanation: The hour hand is nearly at 5 and the minute hand points to 9, which is 45 minutes. The time is 4:45.

questions.questionText: One bottle holds 2 liters of water. How many liters of water do 6 full bottles hold?
questions.explanation: 6 bottles × 2 liters = 12 liters.

questions.questionText: The bar graph shows books read by four students. How many MORE books did Owen read than Mia?
questions.explanation: Owen read 15 books and Mia read 6. 15 − 6 = 9 more books.

questions.questionText: Each ▲ stands for 4 cans collected. How many MORE cans were collected on Tuesday than on Monday?
questions.explanation: Tuesday: 8 × 4 = 32 cans. Monday: 3 × 4 = 12 cans. 32 − 12 = 20 more cans. The distractor 5 is the difference in symbols, not cans.

questions.questionText: Select ALL shapes that are quadrilaterals.
questions.explanation: Squares, rhombuses, and trapezoids all have exactly 4 sides, so they are quadrilaterals. A pentagon has 5 sides.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
